key_insight: DeepJEB++ grows a small set of jet engine brackets into a much larger 3D, simulation-labeled dataset by augmenting in 2D latent space first, then lifting the validated images back to 3D and auto-labeling them.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical route to generate scalable geometry-physics datasets for solid mechanics and design optimization when labeled 3D engineering data are scarce.
method: Fine-tune a pretrained 2D latent diffusion model on multi-view bracket renders and generate new views via latent interpolation.
Use a vision-language-model quality filter to keep only manufacturable designs.
Lift validated images to 3D meshes with a domain-adapted generative foundation model, then automatically assign mass, stress, and displacement labels from detected load/bolt interfaces.
evidence_signal: Starting from fewer than 400 seed designs, the pipeline produces 15,360 simulation-labeled 3D brackets, a 40x expansion, and evaluates quality by manufacturability, label fidelity to SimJEB ground truth, and distributional consistency.
watch_item: The workflow depends on robust interface recognition and cross-stage consistency; errors in 2D-to-3D transfer or automated boundary-condition assignment could propagate into the final labels.
paper_title: DeepJEB++: Foundation Model-Driven Large-Scale 3D Engineering Dataset via 2D Latent Space Augmentation
